source_text: That evening I spoke at length with my mother.

reference_translations.text: Quella sera parlai a lungo con mia madre.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. -are io passato remoto parlai; subject pronoun dropped.

reference_translations.text: Quella sera parlavo a lungo con mia madre.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG tense: imperfect parlavo gives a habit, not the single completed event.

explanation: 'That evening' frames a single completed past, so Italian written narrative takes the passato remoto. Regular -are gives the io form parlai. The imperfect parlavo would say 'I used to speak', a different meaning.

source_text: No one believed us at first.

reference_translations.text: Nessuno ci credette all'inizio.
reference_translations.notes: Standard, -etti pattern.

reference_translations.text: Nessuno ci credé all'inizio.
reference_translations.notes: Equally standard, -ei pattern (3sg credé with accent).

reference_translations.text: Nessuno ci credeva all'inizio.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG tense: imperfect credeva.

explanation: Regular -ere verbs have two accepted lui/lei forms in the passato remoto, credé or credette; both are correct here. The imperfect credeva would describe an ongoing disbelief rather than stating it as a completed fact.

source_text: That night I did not sleep at all.

reference_translations.text: Quella notte non dormii affatto.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. -ire io passato remoto dormii, double i.

reference_translations.text: Quella notte non dormivo affatto.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG tense: imperfect.

explanation: Regular -ire builds the io passato remoto with -ii, giving the written double i dormii. 'That night' marks one finished night, so the remote past, not the imperfect dormivo.

source_text: That morning I took the first train to Rome.

reference_translations.text: Quella mattina presi il primo treno per Roma.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Strong stem: prendere -> presi.

reference_translations.text: Quella mattina prendetti il primo treno per Roma.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: regularised, ignores the strong stem.

explanation: prendere has the irregular strong stem pres- in the io form: presi. Regularising it to prendei/prendetti is the signature miss for this large -ere class.

source_text: Manzoni wrote his great novel in the nineteenth century.

reference_translations.text: Manzoni scrisse il suo grande romanzo nell'Ottocento.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Strong stem scriss-, double s.

reference_translations.text: Manzoni scriveva il suo grande romanzo nell'Ottocento.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG tense: imperfect.

explanation: scrivere takes the strong stem scriss- in the lui/lei form scrisse. Biographical and historical statement is exactly where Italian uses the passato remoto.

source_text: My grandparents lived in the country for many years.

reference_translations.text: I miei nonni vissero in campagna per molti anni.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Strong stem viss-, loro form vissero.

reference_translations.text: I miei nonni vivevano in campagna per molti anni.
reference_translations.notes: Acceptable only as an imperfect 'used to live'; the bounded 'for many years' completed stretch wants the remote past.

explanation: vivere has the strong stem viss-: the loro form is vissero. A bounded completed stretch ('for many years', now over) takes the remote past in narrative; the imperfect would background it as an ongoing habit.

source_text: When did you all come to Rome for the first time?

reference_translations.text: Quando veniste a Roma per la prima volta?
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Regular middle person: venire -> veniste (voi), not venneste.

reference_translations.text: Quando venneste a Roma per la prima volta?
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: strong stem carried into the regular voi person.

explanation: venire follows the strong pattern, but only in three persons. The voi form is regular: veniste, on the ordinary stem. Carrying the strong stem venn- into the middle persons (venneste) is the diagnostic error this item tests.

source_text: The journey was long and difficult.

reference_translations.text: Il viaggio fu lungo e difficile.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. Suppletive essere: fu.

reference_translations.text: Il viaggio era lungo e difficile.
reference_translations.notes: Imperfect reading 'was (ongoing)'; the remote past states it as a completed whole, the narrative default here.

explanation: essere is suppletive in the passato remoto: the lui/lei form is fu, one of the commonest narrative words. It frames the journey as a single completed whole; the imperfect era would background it.

source_text: At that moment I was very afraid.

reference_translations.text: In quel momento ebbi una gran paura.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. avere strong stem ebb-: ebbi paura.

reference_translations.text: In quel momento avevo una gran paura.
reference_translations.notes: Imperfect 'I was (ongoing) afraid'; 'at that moment' points to a single onset, the remote past ebbi.

explanation: Italian says 'avere paura' (to have fear). avere takes the strong stem ebb- in the io form ebbi. 'At that moment' marks a single point of onset, so the remote past, not the ongoing imperfect avevo.

source_text: That day she made a difficult choice.

reference_translations.text: Quel giorno fece una scelta difficile.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. fare strong stem fec-: fece.

reference_translations.text: Quel giorno faceva una scelta difficile.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG tense: imperfect.

explanation: fare has the strong stem fec-: the lui/lei form is fece. 'That day' plus a single decision pins the completed remote past.

source_text: He did not say a single word all evening.

reference_translations.text: Non disse una sola parola in tutta la sera.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. dire strong stem diss-: disse.

reference_translations.text: Non diceva una sola parola in tutta la sera.
reference_translations.notes: Imperfect 'was not saying'; the completed whole evening wants the remote past disse.

explanation: dire takes the strong stem diss- in the lui/lei form disse. The completed, bounded evening is narrated with the remote past.

source_text: As soon as he had finished speaking, everyone applauded.

reference_translations.text: Appena ebbe finito di parlare, tutti applaudirono.
reference_translations.notes: Standard trapassato remoto: ebbe finito, with the main clause in the passato remoto.

reference_translations.text: Appena aveva finito di parlare, tutti applaudirono.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: pluperfect aveva finito; the literary 'appena + passato remoto main clause' frame wants the trapassato remoto.

explanation: The trapassato remoto places a completed action just before a remote-past main clause, after 'appena' (as soon as), 'dopo che', or 'quando'. It is the passato remoto of the auxiliary (ebbe) plus the participle (finito). Highly literary; in everyday Italian the pluperfect aveva finito or even the passato prossimo would stand in.

source_text: After he had left, we regretted the quarrel.

reference_translations.text: Dopo che fu partito, ci pentimmo della lite.
reference_translations.notes: Standard. partire takes essere: fu partito; participle agrees m.sg.

reference_translations.text: Dopo che era partito, ci pentimmo della lite.
reference_translations.notes: WRONG: pluperfect era partito instead of the remote fu partito.

explanation: partire is a verb of motion, so the trapassato remoto takes essere: fu partito, the participle agreeing with the masculine singular subject. 'Dopo che ... pentimmo' (a remote-past main clause) is the frame; the pluperfect era partito is the everyday equivalent.
